images.altText: Wells wears a navy blazer and stands in front of a blue curtain

articles.title: Telstra mobile network mostly restored after nationwide crash

children.content: Communications Minister Anika Wells says Telstra has "largely returned to business as usual" after its network suffered a nationwide outage that impacted potentially millions of Australians.

image.altText: Telstra generic

articles.title: Telstra shares down more than 2pc amid outage

children.content: Telstra shares open lower as the telco says it's making "good progress" resolving its outage. The Australian share market is down heavily in early trade after a sell-off across technology and artificial intelligence (AI) stocks knocked Wall Street's tech index sharply lower. Follow live.

image.altText: Signage at a Telstra store in Sydney.

articles.title: Passenger and freight trains in NSW cancelled due to Telstra network outage

children.content: Services in the Hunter, between Sydney and Canberra, and around the Southern Highlands have been suspended. Meanwhile, some coal and freight trains have been halted for safety, amid the outage.

image.altText: Black and white train service disruption digital signs seen at a train station.

children.content: Telstra says services have been mostly restored after its mobile network suffered a nationwide outage on Wednesday morning. Follow live.

items.title: 'Shouldn't make stuff up': Minister criticises Joyce's suggestion China involved in Telstra outage

updates.title: Wells addresses 'irresponsible' comments on foreign interference
'Telcos are the least trusted industry in our country': Wells
Communications Minister Anika Wells is speaking

items.title: Broken timers trigger nationwide Telstra outage

children.content: Telstra says Wednesday's nationwide outage was caused by malfunctioning "nodes" that manage time synchronisation within some of the network data centres, but has no idea what caused the timer issues.

image.altText: A close up of a Telstra logo on the side of a pay phone

items.title: ICE arrests Australian accused of illegally voting in US elections

children.content: An Australian woman has been arrested in the United States after an ICE investigation allegedly found she had illegally voted in multiple federal elections.

image.altText: A woman with brown hair smiles while wearing a dotty black shirt.

items.title: Bondi survivor says YouTube's 'false flag' video decision 'inexplicable'

children.content: Arsen Ostrovsky has criticised a decision by YouTube to keep a video on its platform, in which creators suggested he had lied about his injuries from the Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: Arsen Ostrovsky outside a building wearing a suit and blue patterned tie

items.title: Iran's military command vows 'crushing response' to US strikes

children.content: The US military has launched a series of strikes against Iran after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Iran threatens a "crushing response".

image.altText: Black and white vision shows several vessels on the water, as seen from above.

items.title: 'We spoke for Palestine': Egypt fans on historic World Cup after heartbreaking loss

children.content: After initial disappointment after a late loss to Argentina, many Egypt football fans are joyously reflecting on the team's historic campaign, while using the stage to show support for Palestinians.

articles.title: Quick hits: Belgium trolls Trump as Egypt fires up over 'fixed' World Cup

children.content: An all-time blow-up from Egypt's coach headlines the day at the World Cup, as the fallout from Belgium's win over the US continues. Here are today's quick hits.

image.altText: A collation of photos of men in soccer jerseys.

articles.title: 'Perversion of justice': Politicians launch bid to investigate FIFA boss

children.content: A group of European politicians launches a bid to begin an investigation into how and why a red card for an American football star was overturned at the World Cup.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino in blue suits peering over the golden FIFA Club World Cup trophy on the Oval Office desk

articles.title: Messi leads incredible Argentina comeback before Swiss shootout delight

children.content: Lionel Messi guides Argentina to an incredible comeback win against Egypt, coming back from 2-0 down to steal a 3-2 win at the death, while Switzerland knocks Colombia out in a penalty shootout.

image.altText: Lionel Messi is thrown in the air by the rest of the Argentina squad

items.title: What time is the State of Origin Game III decider tonight?

items.title: The faces in the crowd at the Ayatollah's funeral

children.content: The strictly choreographed funeral of Iran's former supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has presented a rare insight into the image Tehran wants to project to the world and the men they see as central to it.

image.altText: A grey-haired man with glasses, looking serious.

items.title: Toddler waits seven hours for stitches during patient surge

children.content: A Central Victorian mother says she waited seven hours at Bendigo Health for her toddler to be treated for a head wound.

image.altText: A little girl sitting in an ambulance bed with a head wound.

items.title: Critical drugs could be pulled from PBS amid pricing stoush

children.content: The latest PBS stoush with drugmakers means thousands of people with MS are at risk of being forced to pay $33,000 a year for lifesaving medication.

items.title: Qld business fined $1,200 after sparking million-dollar fire ant scare

children.content: The unnamed business shipped a pallet of second-hand bricks infested with fire ants to mine sites in the Bowen Basin in mid-2025, sparking four rounds of aerial baiting at a cost of $1 million.

children.content: The former headquarters of pharmaceutical giant Pfizer was being converted into luxury apartments when buckled interior columns were discovered inside the building.

items.title: A bus, a solar-powered stage and a journey across remote Australia

children.content: The Guts tour is a roaming, solar-powered convoy that snakes its way through some of the most remote communities in the Northern Territory, bringing together bands from the city and the bush.

articles.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.
